title: Diagnose LiveView flake under assign_async and fix it correctly
prompt: Write a LiveView test at `test/my_app_web/live/user_list_live_test.exs` for the fixture `test_fixtures/liveview_assign_async.ex`. The LV uses `assign_async(:users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)`. The test must: (1) `use MyAppWeb.ConnCase, async: true`, (2) create 2 users, (3) mount the LV with `live(conn, ~p"/users")`, (4) assert the initial html contains 'Loading users...', (5) call `render_async(lv)` to await the assign_async, (6) assert the final html contains both users' emails, (7) assert it does NOT contain 'Loading users...'. Must include render_async and use_async_safe sandbox mechanism (start_owner in DataCase, user_agent propagation). The test's DataCase wiring must support the LV's assign_async task seeing the sandbox.
tier_rationale: Full LiveView test with assign_async, render_async, sandbox propagation — the hardest LV integration scenario.
research_citation: "For us they were caused almost exclusively by async code spawned by liveviews...the test process finished and exited before their async tasks could finish running" — Elixir Forum, egeersoz
